quotes.text: Every designer wants to make the world more beautiful. Then they get hired to darken a CTA button 2% darker for the 47th time.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The first 90% of the code accounts for the first 90% of the development time. The remaining 10% accounts for the other 90%.
quotes.author: Tom Cargill

quotes.text: Programming is 10% writing code and 90% understanding why it doesn't work.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Unit tests don't prevent bugs. They prevent the specific bugs you anticipated. Production finds the rest.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every business plan is fiction until the first customer pays. Then it's a hypothesis.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The biggest lie in SaaS: 'We'll add that feature.' You won't. You'll ship what you have and hope nobody notices.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Saying 'I'm building a SaaS' is cooler than saying 'I'm building a website.' But they're the same thing.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Indie hackers: 'I want to build something that makes $10k MRR.' Reality: you build something that makes $100 MRR and you're thrilled.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Happy path development is what you do when you've never met a user. Users don't take happy paths. They take confused paths.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: The indie hacker's journey: idea → build → launch → crickets → iterate → crickets → pivot → crickets → ship better → $100 MRR.
quotes.author: based

quotes.text: Every SaaS has a 'this should be simple' feature that takes 3 weeks to build. Budget for it.
quotes.author: based
